compiled_state.title: Onboarding skill fails to install DSO commit hooks (test gate, review gate, auto-format)
compiled_state.author: Joe Oakhart

comments.body: The /dso:onboarding skill fails to install required DSO commit hooks (pre-commit-test-gate.sh, pre-commit-review-gate.sh, auto-format) in the host project. When asked whether commit hooks were configured, the agent incorrectly stated these are only Claude Code tool-use hooks and not git commit hooks — but the DSO architecture explicitly uses a two-layer defense-in-depth model where Layer 1 is git pre-commit hooks and Layer 2 is Claude Code PreToolUse hooks. Both layers are required. The agent confused the two layers and skipped Layer 1 entirely. The onboarding skill should: (1) Install pre-commit-test-gate.sh and pre-commit-review-gate.sh as git pre-commit hooks in the host project (integrating with existing hook managers like Husky if present). (2) Configure the auto-format PostToolUse hook. (3) Not treat these as optional or ask whether the user wants them — they are core DSO enforcement infrastructure. The two-layer model exists precisely so that enforcement cannot be bypassed by either git or Claude Code alone.
comments.author: Joe Oakhart

comments.body:
Beta testing session findings (parity-scan project, Node/TypeScript + Husky):

HOOKS THAT SHOULD BE INSTALLED:
1. pre-commit-test-gate.sh (git pre-commit hook, Layer 1) — blocks commits when staged source files have untested changes
2. pre-commit-review-gate.sh (git pre-commit hook, Layer 1) — blocks commits with non-allowlisted files lacking review
3. pre-commit-executable-guard.sh — prevents .sh files from losing executable bit
4. pre-commit-wrapper.sh — timeout enforcement wrapper for all hook commands (hard-kill limits)
5. pre-commit-format-fix.sh — Python-only auto-format, should be SKIPPED for non-Python projects

INTEGRATION PATTERN (Husky example):
The hooks should be called from the project's existing hook manager (Husky .husky/pre-commit, pre-commit framework, etc.) rather than replacing it. Each hook is wrapped with the timeout wrapper and given appropriate limits:
- Project tests: 60s
- Lint-staged: 30s  
- Executable guard: 5s
- Test gate: 10s
- Review gate: 10s

PATH RESOLUTION:
Hooks resolve via DSO_ROOT variable: ${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT:-$HOME/.claude/plugins/marketplaces/digital-service-orchestra/plugins/dso}. The pre-commit-test-gate.sh and pre-commit-review-gate.sh resolve their own dependencies via HOOK_DIR relative to their location — they work when called directly from the plugin path.

GRACEFUL FALLBACK:
When DSO plugin is not installed (wrapper not found), the hook should fall back to running the project's original commands without timeout wrapping or DSO gates. This ensures the project remains functional without the plugin.

KEY INSIGHT:
The onboarding skill needs to detect the host project's hook manager (Husky, pre-commit, bare .git/hooks) and integrate accordingly rather than assuming a single installation method.
comments.author: Joe Oakhart
